problem: helix-cli (v1.2.0) lacks README documentation, has no automated npm publish workflow, has no artifact retrieval commands, and contains a version string mismatch (package.json says 1.2.0, src/index.ts hardcodes 0.1.0). The CLI currently supports login, inspect, and comments commands but no artifact access.

files.why: Defines package name (@projectxinnovation/helix-cli), version (1.2.0), bin entry (dist/index.js), build scripts (tsc), engines (>=18), files (dist only), and devDependencies (typescript, @types/node). No publishConfig or prepublishOnly script present.
TypeScript build configuration: ES2022 target, Node16 module/resolution, strict mode, outputs to dist/ with declarations.
CLI entrypoint with shebang. Routes to login/inspect/comments commands. Hardcodes version as '0.1.0' (line 47) despite package.json saying 1.2.0. New 'artifacts' command branch needed here.

Config loading: env vars (HELIX_API_KEY/HELIX_INSPECT_TOKEN/HELIX_INSPECT_API_KEY, HELIX_URL/HELIX_INSPECT_BASE_URL/HELIX_INSPECT_URL) take priority, then ~/.hlx/config.json. Exports HxConfig type {apiKey, url}.

HTTP client (hxFetch) with retry logic (3 attempts, exponential backoff). Sends hxi_ keys as X-API-Key header, other tokens as Bearer. Default basePath is /api/inspect, overridable per call. This is the transport layer artifact commands will use.
OAuth flow (local server callback) and manual API key entry. OAuth returns a 'key' from the web client callback. Manual mode expects hxi_* format. Saves to ~/.hlx/config.json.
Inspect command router (repos/db/logs/api). Pattern to follow for new artifacts command module.
Comments command router (list/post). Uses basePath '/api' instead of default '/api/inspect'. Resolves ticket ID from --ticket flag or HELIX_TICKET_ID env var.
Comments list implementation showing existing pattern: typed response, hxFetch with basePath override, client-side filtering, human-readable output.
Ignores node_modules/ and dist/. No dist/ directory exists in repo (built artifact is not committed).

facts: Package name is @projectxinnovation/helix-cli version 1.2.0, but CLI --version outputs '0.1.0' (src/index.ts line 47)
No README.md exists at repository root
No .github/workflows/ directory exists - no CI/CD workflows present
No dist/ directory exists in repo - built artifacts are gitignored
No test files exist anywhere in the codebase; no test script in package.json
Only devDependencies: typescript ^6.0.2 and @types/node ^25.5.0; zero production dependencies (CLI uses only Node.js builtins)
bin entry points to dist/index.js - published package must include compiled output
files array restricts npm package to 'dist' directory only
Package is ESM-only (type: module) targeting Node >=18
Build is simple tsc; typecheck is tsc --noEmit; no lint command exists
CLI uses custom arg parsing (no commander/yargs) - simple switch/case routing in index.ts
CLI auth sends hxi_ prefixed keys via X-API-Key header, other tokens via Authorization: Bearer header
Comments commands use basePath '/api' (session/inspection auth), inspect commands use default basePath '/api/inspect'

Server artifact endpoints (GET /tickets/:ticketId/artifacts, GET /tickets/:ticketId/runs/:runId/step-artifacts/:stepId) are behind requireAuth gate (session auth via attachAuthContext), NOT behind attachInspectionAuth
Server inspection and comment endpoints are before the requireAuth gate and use attachInspectionAuth which accepts hxi_ API keys
Server's attachAuthContext only recognizes session JWTs (Bearer tokens verified by verifyAccessToken), NOT hxi_ inspection API keys
Server's attachInspectionAuth recognizes hxi_ keys (X-API-Key or Bearer), inspection tokens (scoped JWT), and session JWTs
Artifact data is stored in Vercel Blob (private access) at paths like artifacts/{runId}/{repoKey}/{stepId}/{filename}
GitHub artifact URLs are optionally built from committed branch artifacts (controlled by org.commitArtifactsToGithub setting)
GET /tickets/:ticketId/artifacts returns {items: [{id, label, repoUrl, runId, branch, path, url}], stepArtifactSummary: [{stepId, repoKey}]}
GET /tickets/:ticketId/runs/:runId/step-artifacts/:stepId?repoKey=X returns {stepId, repoKey, files: [{name, content, contentType}]}
HELIX_TICKET_ID env var is already used by comments commands for ticket resolution
unknowns: What format does the OAuth login flow return - session JWT or hxi_ API key? This determines whether artifact endpoints are accessible with OAuth-obtained credentials.
Is the published npm package @projectxinnovation/helix-cli on a public or private npm registry? No publishConfig in package.json to indicate registry.
What npm auth secret name should the GitHub Actions workflow use? No existing workflow or documentation specifies this.

Should artifact endpoints in the server be moved before the requireAuth gate (like comments) to support inspection token auth, or should the CLI only support OAuth-obtained session tokens for artifact access?

Does the ticket's reference to 'GitHub Actions artifacts' mean the GitHub Actions artifact API, or the Helix artifacts committed to GitHub branches? The actual implementation uses Vercel Blob + optional GitHub branch commits, not the Actions artifact API.
Is there an existing published npm package version that could indicate drift? The package.json says 1.2.0 but the CLI outputs 0.1.0.
The HELIX_RUN_ID env var is not currently used by any command - should artifact commands support it alongside --run flag?
Should artifact retrieval support both human-readable and --json output modes from the start?
